sections.paragraph:
δάκνω fut. δήξομαι LXX; 2 aor. ἔδακον (LXX, Just., D. 91, 4; 131, 4). Pass. 1 aor. ἐδήχθην, subj. δηχθῶ ; pf. ptc. δεδηγμένος (Just., D. 112, 1) (Hom. et al.; LXX, Just.)

① to cause harm by biting, bite of snakes B 12:5. Pass. (Diog. L. 5, 78 ὑπʼ ἀσπίδος δηχθείς) vs. 7 (cp. Num 21:6ff).

② to cause discomfort to, harm, fig. ext. of 1 (Hdt. 7, 16, 1; X., Cyr. 4, 3, 3; Epict. 2, 22, 28 δάκνειν ἀλλήλους καὶ λοιδορεῖσθαι; Appian, Syr. 10 §40=offend, nettle; PBrem 56, app. 11; Hab 2:7; Philo, Leg. All. 2, 8) w. κατεσθίειν (q.v.) Gal 5:15.—B. 266f. DELG. M-M.

fut. fut. = fut.

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

aor. aor. = aorist

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

subj. subj. = subjunctive; subject

  1. pf. = perfect

ptc. ptc. = participle

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

Diog. L. Diog. L. = Diogenes Laertius, III a.d.—List 5

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

ff ff = (and) following

ext. ext. = extension

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

Appian Appian , I–II a.d.—List 5

Syr. Syr. = Syriac

PBrem PBrem = Die Bremer Papyri—List 4

app. app. = appendix, apparatus (when ref. to marginal text-critical information, esp. in N.)

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

q.v. q.v. = quod vide (which see, see that which has just been mentioned)

  1. B. = CDBuck, A Dictionary of Selected Synonyms—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 211
